153.content:
A pair of immensely wealthy college students take their friends on a cruise with their private boat in the South Pacific. Beth thinks they're going to the Bahamas, but her brother, Adam, and their captain, Harvey, have a more adventurous idea in mind. They've found coordinates for a mysterious, unclaimed island and plan on taking their friends on unforgettable experience. Little do they know, the island is very much claimed by something much larger.

Characters involved:

-Adam, 22, dark brown hair, medium build. Basically your late-90s, early 2000s Disney protagonist.

-Harvey, 31, black, slightly above average build. Practically spends half his life on or in the water. Fancies himself to be a Bear Grylls tier survivalist.

-Beth, 20, dyed blue hair, slightly on the thicc side. Wants to be a good host and was hoping to use this vacation to seduce Leon. More practically minded than her brother, Adam.

-Leon, 20, black, the starting running back for their college's football team. Built like an action figure and knows the effect he has on women. Knows that Beth is into him, but wants to see what kind of game the rich girl has.

-Kelly, 19, sandy blond hair, very tall, the midblocker for their college's volleyball team. If she had the self confidence, she could probably get into modeling, but after years of towering over everyone around her she developed a shy personality. She considers Beth to be one of her best friends and the last thing she'd want to do is take Leon's interest away from Beth.

-Emily, 20, red-head, very short. Coming from a poor family and getting into college through her book smarts, friendship with her rich friend Adam has been quite the adventure. She can tell that he has a crush on her, but she herself can't tell if she likes him for him or for his lifestyle. Despite her inquisitive nature, she's liked by everyone on board.

Now that we have our group, what does Konga look like?

"This doesn't exactly look like the Bahamas," Beth yells at her brother. "In fact, I'm pretty sure we're in the wrong body of water."
"Beth, did you pay any attention in school?" Adam says.
"That's not the point," Beth says.
"Look," Harvey says, "we're already here. We may as well make ourselves at home."
"Where is 'here' anyway?" Leon asks.
"Good question," Kelly says.
Indeed, they find themselves on the shore of a seemingly deserted island. From what little they saw, it is well within international waters and not claimed by any known government. To the men, it seems like an adventure. To most of the women, it seems like an inconvenience. To Emily, it could possibly be the chance of a lifetime.
Unfortunately, to the island's inhabitants, it is an intrusion. As no one has ever escaped the island alive, the natives are unknown to the outside world. The college kids have no idea that they have landed on an island of giants.
15332.title: Island of the Gods

info.brief_description: A shipwreck victim lands on an island, encounters a giant that natives worship. New edits
info.description:
This is essentially "King Kong" with a giant human instead of the gorilla. I have seen similar stories on this site, but they were only about either teens or pre-teens. I wanted to do something that actually involved adults for a switch.
The only rule is that the chapters must be coherent. That means that I don't want to see run-on sentences or bad punctuation. At least show a basic knowledge of grammar. Other than that, go nuts.
On suggestion of a reviewer, I adjusted the age ranges of some of my chapters. NO MORE UNDERAGE ALLOWED! I want this to be as graphic as you can make it.
